reflection:
Now I have everything I need to analyze the game and compare it to the existing strategy.

**Game Analysis:**

| # | Guess | Result |
|---|-------|--------|
| 1 | CRANE | C⬛ R🟨 A⬛ N🟨 E🟨 |
| 2 | STERN | S⬛ T🟨 E🟨 R🟩 N🟨 |
| 3 | ENTRY | ✅ Solved! |

**What went well:**
- **CRANE** as opener: hit 3 yellows (R, N, E), eliminated C and A — strong yield
- **STERN** as guess 2 was excellent: it repositioned *all three* yellows from guess 1 into fresh positions simultaneously, while also testing two new letters (S, T). R went green at position 4, and T was confirmed in-word
- **ENTRY** as guess 3: the tight constraints (4 confirmed letters, 1 green) made the answer nearly singular — the player deduced it cleanly using positional reasoning

**One gap identified:** The strategy talks about avoiding absent letters in guess 2 and testing "fresh high-frequency letters," but doesn't explicitly call out the high-value tactic demonstrated here — repositioning **all** yellow letters simultaneously in a single guess. STERN placed R, E, and N all in new positions at once, maximising positional information per guess.

The rest of the strategy is well-validated by this game. I'll add one targeted principle to the Opening Strategy section:

---

**Game Summary:**

This was a clean, efficient **3-guess solve** of the word **ENTRY**. The play quality was high throughout:

- **CRANE** (guess 1): Strong opener — yielded 3 yellows (R, N, E) and eliminated C and A
- **STERN** (guess 2): Excellent — repositioned all three yellows from guess 1 into new positions simultaneously, confirmed R green at position 4, and added T as a confirmed letter
- **ENTRY** (guess 3): Perfect constraint-based deduction — with E locked to positions 1–2, N to 1–3, T to 1/3/5, and R green at 4, ENTRY was the natural and unique solution

**Strategy changes made:**
One new principle was added to the **Opening Strategy** section:

> **Reposition ALL yellows in guess 2** — when guess 1 yields multiple yellow letters, choose guess 2 so that *every* yellow is moved to a new, valid position in one guess. This game demonstrated it perfectly: CRANE's R🟨 N🟨 E🟨 were all repositioned by STERN in one move, maximising positional information per guess.

A second minor addition: after guess 2, explicitly check if the constraints already point to a single candidate word — if so, commit immediately rather than playing another exploratory turn.
